raw_json: {"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"biological_fact","confidence":0.91,"matched_text":"the toxins that mussels produce to protect themselves from predators can have various effects on the environment.","distilled_fact":"Toxins that mussels produce to protect themselves from predators can have various effects on the environment."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"biological_fact","confidence":0.88,"matched_text":"some mussels release chemicals that act as pesticides to deter herbivorous animals from consuming the algae or plants on which the mussels, and other organisms, depend for their food.","distilled_fact":"Some mussels release chemicals that act as pesticides to deter herbivorous animals from consuming the algae or plants on which the mussels and other organisms depend for their food."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"biological_fact","confidence":0.86,"matched_text":"these chemical defense mechanisms can also affect other organisms in the ecosystem, such as reducing the growth or survival of potential competitors or predators.","distilled_fact":"These chemical defense mechanisms can also affect other organisms in the ecosystem by reducing the growth or survival of potential competitors or predators."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"biological_fact","confidence":0.87,"matched_text":"the high concentrations of toxins from dense mussel populations can be toxic to fish or other organisms that consume them, leading to negative impacts on the food web.","distilled_fact":"High concentrations of toxins from dense mussel populations can be toxic to fish or other organisms that consume them, leading to negative impacts on the food web."}]}
